spec: RFC-0008 §4.2 (Verifiable Random Function); RFC 9381 ECVRF-EDWARDS25519-SHA512-ELL2 (ciphersuite suite_string = 0x04)
vendor: in-tree implementation in ants-client/foundation/crypto, built on libsodium 1.0.22 ref10 fe25519/ge25519/sc25519 primitives; hash-to-curve uses the RFC 9380 ELL2_NU suite edwards25519_XMD:SHA-512_ELL2_NU_
notes: All three positive vectors are copied verbatim from RFC 9381 §B.4 Examples 19, 20, 21. The 32-byte 'sk' is the same Ed25519 seed used in RFC 8032 §7.1; pi is 80 bytes (Gamma || c[16] || s[32]); beta is the 64-byte SHA-512 output. The implementation passes these byte-for-byte against the in-tree test_crypto suite.

negative_vectors.name: tampered Gamma (bit-flip in pi[0])
negative_vectors.notes: Decompression of the tampered Gamma either yields a non-curve point or a different point whose subsequent challenge doesn't match c_string.

negative_vectors.name: tampered challenge (bit-flip in pi[32])
negative_vectors.notes: Tampered c causes the reconstructed c' to mismatch.

negative_vectors.name: tampered response s (bit-flip in pi[48])
negative_vectors.notes: Tampered s shifts U, V and the reconstructed c' mismatches.

negative_vectors.name: tampered alpha (bit-flip in alpha[0])
negative_vectors.notes: Changing alpha changes the hashed-to-curve point H, breaking verification.

negative_vectors.name: tampered PK (bit-flip in pk[0])
negative_vectors.notes: Tampered PK either becomes non-canonical (rejected at the canonicalness check) or yields a different Y that breaks the verification equation.
